message.content:
You are an adversarial verification agent. The lens you check through: credentials-and-history.

Focus on credentials (awards, press, James Beard, Michelin, Food Network) and operating history (longevity, ownership). Try to REFUTE each claim. Look up each award independently — James Beard winners and nominees are listed in primary sources at jamesbeard.org. Michelin is listed at guide.michelin.com. Cross-check year-by-year.

Entity under review: Fisherman's Market & Grill (La Quinta) (La Quinta)

Claimed assessment (verify or refute these specific claims):
- Review volume: Yelp 744 reviews (4.0 stars), Tripadvisor 401 reviews (4.2 stars, #11 of 127 La Quinta restaurants); Google count [UNVERIFIED] but likely 1000+ given multi-platform footprint
- Sentiment: mostly positive
- Longevity: Founded 2002 by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz; ~24 years operating; ownership appears stable across three Coachella Valley locations (Palm Springs, La Quinta, Palm Desert)
- Credentials: ["Repeatedly cited as 'Best Seafood' and 'Best Fish Tacos' in Coachella Valley local press / readers' polls (specific publication and year [UNVERIFIED] - claims of '16 consecutive years #1' appear in marketing copy but original award source not confirmed)","Shanghai Reds (sister concept under same ownership) named perennial honoree in Desert Magazine 'Best of the Valley' competition per Coachella Valley Independent"]
- Appears in queries: Surfaces reliably for 'best fish tacos Coachella Valley/Palm Desert/La Quinta' and 'best seafood Coachella Valley' lists. Does NOT consistently appear in 'fine dining La Quinta' (loses to LG's Prime, Cliffhouse, Mitch's on El Paseo) or 'date night Rancho Mirage' (loses to Oceana, Catalan, The Edge, Wally's). Casual seafood category-defender, not fine-dining contender.
- Site structured data: fishermans.com is a Squarespace/Owner-platform site with location pages, embedded maps (lat/lon present), and explicit human-readable hours per location, but NO visible JSON-LD schema.org Restaurant markup detected. No itemprop microdata, no menu schema, no aggregateRating markup. Hours and menus are readable as text but not machine-structured for agents.
- Visibility gap score: 6
- Convertibility tier: convertible

Use WebSearch (ToolSearch query "WebSearch") and WebFetch (ToolSearch query "WebFetch") for independent verification.

Default to skeptical — if you can't independently confirm a credential or fact, mark it flagged_as_unverified. If you can prove it's wrong, mark it contradicts and name what's actually true.

Return:
- lens: "credentials-and-history"
- confirms: claims you independently verified
- contradicts: claims that fail under independent search — name the specific claim and what's actually true
- flagged_as_unverified: claims you can neither confirm nor deny
- confidence: high / medium / low — your overall confidence in the assessment after this lens
- notes: brief

This is adversarial. Refuting matters more than confirming.

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- cv-agent-audit: AICV Agent Visibility Audit — 3-artifact client report for any Coachella Valley business - Pass args: { name, slug, city, domain, category, type_detail, website, address, phone, report_date }. Produces owner report, developer brief, and AICV node YAML seed.
- anthropic-skills:consolidate-memory: Reflective pass over your memory files — merge duplicates, fix stale facts, prune the index.
- anthropic-skills:docx: Use this skill whenever the user wants to create, read, edit, or manipulate Word documents (.docx files). Triggers include: any mention of 'Word doc', 'word document', '.docx', or requests to produce professional documents with formatting like tables of contents, headings, page numbers, or letterheads. Also use when extracting or reorganizing content from .docx files, inserting or replacing images in documents, performing find-and-replace in Word files, working with tracked changes or comments, or converting content into a polished Word document. If the user asks for a 'report', 'memo', 'letter', 'template', or similar deliverable as a Word or .docx file, use this skill. Do NOT use for PDFs, spreadsheets, Google Docs, or general coding tasks unrelated to document generation.
- anthropic-skills:pdf: Use this skill whenever the user wants to do anything with PDF files. This includes reading or extracting text/tables from PDFs, combining or merging multiple PDFs into one, splitting PDFs apart, rotating pages, adding watermarks, creating new PDFs, filling PDF forms, encrypting/decrypting PDFs, extracting images, and OCR on scanned PDFs to make them searchable. If the user mentions a .pdf file or asks to produce one, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:pptx: Use this skill any time a .pptx file is involved in any way — as input, output, or both. This includes: creating slide decks, pitch decks, or presentations; reading, parsing, or extracting text from any .pptx file (even if the extracted content will be used elsewhere, like in an email or summary); editing, modifying, or updating existing presentations; combining or splitting slide files; working with templates, layouts, speaker notes, or comments. Trigger whenever the user mentions "deck," "slides," "presentation," or references a .pptx filename, regardless of what they plan to do with the content afterward. If a .pptx file needs to be opened, created, or touched, use this skill.
- anthropic-skills:schedule: Create or update a scheduled task that runs automatically. Use when the user says things like "every day", "each morning", "remind me in an hour", "run this at noon", or wants to reschedule an existing task.
- anthropic-skills:setup-cowork: Guided Cowork setup — install role-matched plugins, connect your tools, try a skill.
- anthropic-skills:skill-creator: Create new skills, modify and improve existing skills, and measure skill performance. Use when users want to create a skill from scratch, edit, or optimize an existing skill, run evals to test a skill, benchmark skill performance with variance analysis, or optimize a skill's description for better triggering accuracy.
- anthropic-skills:sunshine-fm-human-rhythm: Design music, jingles, and spoken word for SunshineFM using the Human Rhythm Engine. Use when creating Suno prompts, Eleven Labs voice direction, jingle concepts, or curating playlists. Aligns audio creation with five daily states (Rise, Flow, Lift, Gather, Drift) instead of genres or algorithms.
- anthropic-skills:xlsx: Use this skill any time a spreadsheet file is the primary input or output. This means any task where the user wants to: open, read, edit, or fix an existing .xlsx, .xlsm, .csv, or .tsv file (e.g., adding columns, computing formulas, formatting, charting, cleaning messy data); create a new spreadsheet from scratch or from other data sources; or convert between tabular file formats. Trigger especially when the user references a spreadsheet file by name or path — even casually (like "the xlsx in my downloads") — and wants something done to it or produced from it. Also trigger for cleaning or restructuring messy tabular data files (malformed rows, misplaced headers, junk data) into proper spreadsheets. The deliverable must be a spreadsheet file. Do NOT trigger when the primary deliverable is a Word document, HTML report, standalone Python script, database pipeline, or Google Sheets API integration, even if tabular data is involved.
- cowork-plugin-management:cowork-plugin-customizer: Customize a Claude Code plugin for a specific organization's tools and workflows. Use when: customize plugin, set up plugin, configure plugin, tailor plugin, adjust plugin settings, customize plugin connectors, customize plugin skill, tweak plugin, modify plugin configuration.
- cowork-plugin-management:create-cowork-plugin: Guide users through creating a new plugin from scratch in a cowork session. Use when users want to create a plugin, build a plugin, make a new plugin, develop a plugin, scaffold a plugin, start a plugin from scratch, or design a plugin. This skill requires Cowork mode with access to the outputs directory for delivering the final .plugin file.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring remote agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a pull request
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Fisherman's Market Grill and their fish tacos awards:

Fisherman's Market & Grill's Baja Fish Tacos have been voted #1 best fish taco in the Coachella Valley for several years. Additionally, the restaurant has been voted Best "Seafood Restaurant, Value, & Fish Taco".

The recognition extends beyond just local awards. Anthony Bourdain called these fish tacos "Truly Extraordinary", and Rachael Ray visited for Food Network's "$40 a day" program and declared, "In my book, Fisherman's Market & Grill is a hole-in-one!"

The restaurant has multiple locations throughout the Coachella Valley, including Palm Springs, Palm Desert, and La Quinta, making their award-winning fish tacos readily available to residents and visitors in the area.

Based on the search results, here's the history of Fisherman's Market & Grill in La Quinta:

Fisherman's Market & Grill was established by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz in 1995, though some sources indicate the restaurant was established in 2002 at the La Quinta location specifically. Louis Pagano and life-long friend Bill Mortz opened the restaurant 15 years ago with a unique concept.

Two guys with a love for fishing and a passion to share their catch with good friends decided to combine these passions and ask "What would happen if we opened a Fresh Fish Market & Grill, served only the best of the catch in a relaxed atmosphere and did it in... the Desert?"

Louis Pagano grew up on his fishing boat in San Pedro, which gave him expertise in sourcing quality seafood. Louis Pagano and his late partner Bill Mortz came to Palm Springs with a vision to provide the finest quality seafood at an affordable price.

Fisherman's Market & Grill is now four restaurants strong, expanding beyond the original La Quinta location to multiple locations across the Coachella Valley.

Based on the search results, there are conflicting founding dates for Fisherman's Market depending on the location:

**Palm Desert Location:** Established in 1995

**Palm Springs Location:** Established in 2000

The business was founded by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz in 1995, with the original location in Palm Desert. About five years later, Lou and Bill renovated buildings that were built in 1936 and created the second Fisherman's Market & Grill in 1998 in Palm Springs (though one source lists it as 2000).

So to clarify: the original Fisherman's Market was founded in **1995** in Palm Desert, and the Palm Springs location opened a few years later around **1998-2000**.

Based on the search results, I found information about Fisherman's Market's consecutive year awards for best seafood in the Coachella Valley:

Fisherman's Market has been voted the #1 Best Seafood Restaurant for fifteen (15) consecutive years, according to one source from March 2015.

However, a more recent source from April 2026 states that for seventeen consecutive years, the high desert dining public has accorded "best seafood restaurant" honors to the famous seafood market.

So the answer to your query is that Fisherman's Market has been recognized as the best seafood restaurant in the Coachella Valley for either 15 consecutive years (as noted in 2015) or 17 consecutive years (as noted in a more recent 2026 review).

content.text: Yelp count of 744 was confirmed in the initial search result title. Bill Mortz is noted as "late partner" in earlier results - meaning ownership has changed somewhat (he's deceased). Let me verify Bourdain and Rachael Ray claims.

input.query: Rachael Ray "$40 a day" Fisherman's Market Palm Springs Food Network
Anthony Bourdain "Fisherman's Market" Palm Springs fish taco

Based on the search results, I found relevant information about Rachael Ray's "$40 a Day" episode featuring Fisherman's Market in Palm Springs:

Rachael Ray visited Palm Springs, CA for 24 hours and had lunch at The Fisherman's Market and Grille, which was part of her "$40 a Day" Food Network show. The restaurant's $8 lunch menu offers 10 entrees, and the famous fish tacos are served with a side of salsa, which Rachael complimented on the show.

Fisherman's Market & Grill in Palm Springs offers fresh fish in the desert, and it has become a well-known establishment thanks in part to its television exposure on the Food Network.

Based on the search results, here's what I found about Anthony Bourdain and the fish tacos at Fisherman's Market in Palm Springs:

Anthony Bourdain visited Shanghai Reds Bar & Grill (which is located within the Fisherman's Market complex in Palm Springs) in search of a great fish taco, and after eating their world famous Baja Fish Taco, he said "The Fish Taco here is truly extraordinary; truly, truly, extraordinary!"

Shanghai Reds is a small restaurant located down the alley from Fisherman's Market. Shanghai Reds is named for a 1920s waterfront dive bar in San Pedro, California frequented after work by longshoremen.

The tacos feature perfectly fried beer-battered Alaskan cod. The compliment became so famous that the Baja Fish Tacos have been voted #1 best fish taco in the Coachella Valley for several years.

content.text:
Now I have enough to assess. Key findings:

- Founded 2002 by Pagano & Mortz: **CONTRADICTED** - Original Palm Desert location founded ~1993-1995 (NOT 2002). The brand is ~30+ years old, not 24. La Quinta location specifically may have opened later, but founding date claim is wrong.
- Ownership "stable": **PARTIALLY CONTRADICTED** - Bill Mortz is referenced as Pagano's "late partner" (deceased), so ownership did change.
- Yelp 744 reviews 4.0 stars: **CONFIRMED** (visible in search title)
- Tripadvisor 401 reviews, 4.2 stars, #11 of 127: **CONFIRMED exactly**
- Food Network (Rachael Ray $40 a Day): **CONFIRMED** independently via foodnetwork.com listing
- Anthony Bourdain "truly extraordinary" quote: **CONFIRMED** - appears widely in third-party sources (Tripadvisor reviews reference it)
- "Best Seafood" consecutive years awards: **PARTIALLY CONFIRMED** - "17 consecutive years" appears in 2026 source, "15 consecutive years" appears in 2015 source - the award exists but the original publication source (Desert Magazine? KESQ?) isn't pinned to a primary verified citation
- Shanghai Reds Desert Magazine honoree: **FLAGGED** - referenced in Coachella Valley Independent but not pinned to a primary Desert Magazine winners list
- Three CV locations stable: **CONTRADICTED PARTIALLY** - sources say "four restaurants strong" and also note an Acrisure Arena location, suggesting more than three locations

confirms: Yelp 744 reviews with 4.0 stars (confirmed via Yelp listing title)
Tripadvisor 401 reviews, 4.2 stars, #11 of 127 La Quinta restaurants (confirmed exactly on Tripadvisor page)
Featured on Rachael Ray's '$40 a Day' Food Network show (confirmed via Food Network website listing)
Anthony Bourdain 'truly extraordinary' quote about fish tacos (corroborated across multiple independent sources including Tripadvisor reviewer references)
Surfaces consistently in 'best fish tacos Coachella Valley' search queries
Shanghai Reds is a sister concept under same ownership (confirmed via multiple sources including Coachella Valley Independent and Coachella Valley Weekly)
contradicts: Claim that the brand was 'Founded 2002 by Louis Pagano and Bill Mortz' — multiple sources indicate the original Palm Desert location was founded ~1993-1995 by Pagano and Mortz, making the brand ~30+ years old, not ~24. The 2002 date may refer to the La Quinta location opening, but the founding-date claim as stated is incorrect.
Claim of 'ownership appears stable' — Bill Mortz is referenced in multiple sources as Pagano's 'late partner' (i.e., deceased), so the original co-ownership structure no longer exists.

Claim of 'three Coachella Valley locations (Palm Springs, La Quinta, Palm Desert)' — sources state 'four restaurants strong' and reference an Acrisure Arena location (Thousand Palms), suggesting the brand has expanded beyond three CV locations.
flagged_as_unverified: '16 consecutive years #1' Best Seafood claim — sources mention '15 consecutive years' (2015) and '17 consecutive years' (2026), suggesting the streak is real but the original award publication (Desert Magazine? KESQ Best of Coachella Valley? Palm Springs Life?) is not pinned to a primary winners list in any verified citation
Shanghai Reds as 'perennial Desert Magazine Best of the Valley honoree' — cited in Coachella Valley Independent but not directly verified against a Desert Magazine winners archive
Google review count 1000+ — not independently verified in this lens (explicitly marked unverified in original claim)
input.notes: Review counts and platform metrics are accurate. Press credentials (Bourdain, Rachael Ray/Food Network) are real and verifiable in primary sources. The historical/ownership claims are the weakest part of the assessment: founding date is off by roughly a decade, Bill Mortz is deceased (so ownership has shifted), and the location count appears understated. The 'Best Seafood' award streak is real but the awarding publication remains unconfirmed against a primary source. No James Beard, Michelin, or major national-credential claims were made, so nothing to refute there — this is correctly positioned as a local-press-recognized casual seafood operator, not a fine-dining contender.
